revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近露王子]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Monument of CHIKATSUYU-oji remains.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Tanabe|qid=Q304266|1=Tanabe, Wakayama}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.81440996516322|N|135.60562778176126|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Kujūku-ōji|qid=Q3200280|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines}}}}
'''Chikatsuyu Ōji''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview==
'''Chikatsuyu Ōji''' (近露王子) is the former site of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nakahechi, Tanabe|ja|田辺市中辺路町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi, Tanabe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines and part of the {{ill|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|ja|熊野参詣道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野古道|1=Kumano Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17050475}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan on November 2, 2000 (Heisei 12)<ref>{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_kinenbutsu.html|title=Designated Cultural Properties of Tanabe City - Monuments|publisher=Tanabe City Board of Education|accessdate=2009-03-20|qq=}}</ref>. == Overview == After crossing the {{ill|Hashiori Pass|ja|箸折峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiori Pass|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from {{ill|Ōsaka Moto Ōji|ja|大阪本王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka Moto Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524549|1=Ōsakamoto Ōji Shrine}}, Chikatsuyu Ōji is located just to the left of the Kitano Bridge over the {{ill|Hiki River|ja|日置川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiki River|1=Hiki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3093581}}. This shrine is one of the earliest established among the Ninety-Nine Ōji, with its first recorded mention as "Chikayu" appearing in the pilgrimage notes of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamefusa|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623393}} in 1081 (Eihō 1). In 1109 (Tennin 2), {{ill|Fujiwara no Munetada|ja|藤原宗忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Munetada|1=Fujiwara no Munetada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623159}}'s pilgrimage record describes a ritual purification in the river followed by offerings at "Chikatsuyu Ōji." The name "Chikatsuyu" is said to originate from an event during {{ill|Emperor Kazan|en|Emperor Kazan|qid=Q361000|lt=Emperor Kazan|1=Emperor Kazan}}'s pilgrimage to Kumano. While attempting to eat at Hashiori Pass without chopsticks, he improvised using reed stems, which dripped red sap. He remarked, "Is this blood or dew?" ("Chika tsuyu ka?"), giving rise to the name. The current kanji for "Chikatsuyu" first appeared in the pilgrimage notes of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tsuneie|ja|藤原経房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tsuneie|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉田経房|1=Fujiwara Tsunefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11414052}} in 1174 (Jōan 4), and it was also mentioned in {{ill|Fujiwara no Teika|en|Fujiwara no Teika|qid=Q378425|lt=Fujiwara no Teika|1=Fujiwara no Teika}}'s 1201 (Kennin 1) account, ''Kumano Dō no Ma Guki''. These records indicate that poetry gatherings and purification rituals were common at this site, highlighting its role as both a lodging place and a sacred purification site. == Ōji Monument == A stone monument, established in January 1934 (Shōwa 9), stands on the current grounds. The inscription was written by {{ill|Onisaburo Deguchi|en|Onisaburo Deguchi|qid=Q1182931|lt=Deguchi Onisaburō|1=Onisaburo Deguchi}} during his visit on March 20 of the previous year, commissioned by the village mayor Yokoya Tamao. During the 1935 (Shōwa 10) suppression of the {{ill|Oomoto|en|Oomoto|qid=Q385517|lt=Ōmoto religion|1=Oomoto}}, the monument faced potential destruction. Yokoya cleverly submitted a copy of the inscription to the authorities while altering the monument to replace Deguchi's signature with his own, claiming it was merely a replica. As a result, the monument survived and remains a rare example of Deguchi's handwriting<ref>According to an informational plaque installed by the Tanabe City Board of Education.</ref>. == Chikatsuyu Lodgings == Medieval pilgrimage records, such as those by Fujiwara no Teika, describe lodgings in Chikatsuyu. According to Teika, the lodgings were "near the palace, separated by rice fields," and he notes that after a poetry gathering, they crossed the river to visit the Ōji shrine. Records from Emperor Toba's 1134 (Chōshō 3) pilgrimage mention the same lodging, noting that rain from an unseasonal storm leaked through the roof, soaking their clothing. While the exact location of these lodgings is uncertain due to landscape changes caused by a typhoon in 1889 (Meiji 22), a nearby site known as ''Goshoda'' (御所田), near a Yakushi Hall across the river, is speculated to be the original location. == Location == * 906 Kitano, Chikatsuyu, Nakahechi, Tanabe City, Wakayama Prefecture == Notes == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja|author=Ue Toshikatsu|year=2004|title=World Heritage Kumano Kodo|publisher=Shinjuku Shobō|isbn=4880083216|qq=}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Kumaniji Editorial Committee|year=1973|title=Ancient Roads and Ōji Shrines - Kumano Nakahechi|publisher=Kumano Nakahechi Publishing Association|series=Kumano Library 4|qq=}} * {{Cite book|ja|year=1991|title=Kumano Nakahechi - History and Culture|publisher=Kumano Nakahechi Publishing Association|series=Kumano Library Supplement|qq=}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Koyama Yasunori|year=2000|title=Kumano Kodo|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|series=Iwanami Shinsho|isbn=4004306655|qq=}} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Nishi Ritsu|year=1987|title=Kumano Kodo Guide - A Survey of the Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|publisher=Arao Seibundō|series=Minamoto Selection 1|qq=}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Kujuku-ojis|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|en|Kumano Kodō|qid=Q17050475|lt=Kumano Kodō|1=Kumano Kodō}} - {{ill|Nakahechi|ja|中辺路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahechi|1=Nakahechi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30074932}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} ''' ({{ill|Ōsaka Moto Ōji|ja|大阪本王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaka Moto Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524549|1=Ōsakamoto Ōji Shrine}}) * '''Chikatsuyu Ōji''' - ({{ill|Hisohara Ōji|ja|比曽原王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisohara Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九王子 (田辺市中辺路町)|1=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Tanabe City, Nakahechi Town)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370053}}) == External Links == * [http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/000200/photomuseum/088.html Chikatsuyu Ōji] - [http://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/000200/photomuseum/index.html Wakayama Prefecture Photo Museum] {{Shinto Sidebar|qq=}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|近露王子|version=98296007|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[image:Monument of CHIKATSUYU-oji remains.JPG|thumb|200px|The greenstone monument of {{ill|Chikatsuyu Ōji|ja|近露王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsuyu Ōji|1=Chikatsuyu Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11639127}}.]] [[image:CHIKATSUYU-oji monument.JPG|thumb|200px|Ōji Monument, one of the few surviving inscriptions by {{ill|Onisaburo Deguchi|en|Onisaburo Deguchi|qid=Q1182931|lt=Deguchi Onisaburō|1=Onisaburo Deguchi}}.]]
[[ja:近露王子]] [[zh:近露王子]]

(田辺市中辺路町)#大坂本王子|大坂本王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[近露王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[九十九王子 (田辺市中辺路町)#比曽原王子|比曽原王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc" |[[継桜王子]] | style="background:#ccffcc"
